headline: Arkansas outlasts Boston College in offensive showcase despite sloppy execution
body: Arkansas quarterback K.J. Jackson proved why he was named Player of the Game, completing 19 of 34 passes for 288 yards and two touchdowns in a performance that controlled the line of scrimmage despite not being particularly efficient. The Razorbacks' 468 total yards to Boston College's 242 told the story of a team that imposed its will early and never let the Eagles back into the contest, even when their passing game stalled at moments.

Boston College's offense sputtered throughout, managing only 242 yards with quarterback Leavitt completing just 13 of 23 attempts. The Eagles' rushing attack, led by MacCormick's seven carries for 56 yards, failed to generate momentum in a way that could set up play-action or keep Arkansas's defense honest. Leavitt's one touchdown pass wasn't enough to compensate for an offense that couldn't establish rhythm or capitalize on the single turnover Arkansas gifted them.

Arkansas's ground game provided the steady foundation that Jackson needed to operate. Tailback Washington rushed 11 times for 52 yards and a touchdown, keeping Boston College's defense pinned in and opening windows for Jackson's intermediate throws. Wide receiver Parker's 91 yards on just three catches highlighted how effectively the Razorbacks attacked vertically when they dialed up downfield concepts, punishing a Boston College secondary that couldn't sustain coverage.
turningPoint: Arkansas's 188 rushing yards established early dominance that allowed Jackson to operate in manageable third downs, while Boston College's 95 yards on the ground meant the Eagles had to abandon the run game and play predictably into the second half.

unsungHero.name: Stafford
unsungHero.why: Two sacks on a defense that never had to exert itself, Stafford's pressure up the middle prevented Boston College from ever establishing a rhythm and forced hurried decisions that led to the Eagles' conservative offensive approach throughout the game
